200.a: A blood spatter analyst for Miami Metro Police who is secretly a serial killer — he only kills killers

200.q: What is the Dark Passenger?
200.a: Dexter's term for his compulsion to kill

200.q: Who is Dexter's sister?
200.a: Debra Morgan — a foul-mouthed but dedicated detective

200.q: Who is the Ice Truck Killer?
200.a: Brian Moser — revealed to be Dexter's biological brother

200.q: What city is Dexter set in?
Who is Harry Morgan?
200.a: Dexter's adoptive father who taught him the Code — a set of rules for killing only killers

200.q: What is the Code of Harry?
200.a: Only kill people who deserve it and who have slipped through the justice system — and never get caught

200.q: Who is Trinity Killer?
200.a: Arthur Mitchell — a family man who is secretly a meticulous serial killer — played by John Lithgow

200.q: Who does Dexter marry?
200.a: Rita Bennett — a sweet, fragile woman who is murdered by the Trinity Killer

400.q: Who is Lumen Pierce?
400.a: A trauma survivor who becomes Dexter's partner in killing her abusers — Season 5

400.q: What is LaGuerta's arc?
400.a: She becomes suspicious that Dexter is the Bay Harbour Butcher — she is killed by Debra to protect him

400.q: Who is the Doomsday Killer?
400.a: Professor James Gellar and Travis Marshall — religious fanatics recreating apocalyptic tableaus — Season 6

400.q: What is the Debra-Dexter dynamic in later seasons?
400.a: Debra discovers Dexter is a serial killer — she chooses to protect him, which destroys her morally

400.q: Who is Rudy Cooper?
400.a: Brian Moser — the Ice Truck Killer — Dexter's biological brother whom Dexter ultimately kills

400.q: What is the Season 4 ending?
400.a: Trinity kills Rita — Dexter finds Harrison in a pool of blood, mirroring his own childhood trauma

400.q: Who is Detective Quinn?
400.a: A morally flexible detective who becomes suspicious of Dexter but is never able to prove anything

400.q: What is Dexter: New Blood?
400.a: A 2021 revival set in a small New York town where Dexter has been hiding as Jim Lindsay

400.q: What is the significance of Doakes?
400.a: Sergeant Doakes always suspected Dexter — he is framed as the Bay Harbour Butcher and killed

What is the psychological complexity of the Code?
600.a: Dexter channels his psychopathic urges into a moral framework — but the show questions whether this makes him heroic or just a more selective monster

600.q: What is the final fate of Dexter in the original series?
600.a: He fakes his death and becomes a lumberjack in Oregon — one of TV's most controversial finales

600.q: What is the significance of Harrison in New Blood?
600.a: Dexter's son inherited the Dark Passenger — forcing Dexter to confront what he has passed on

600.q: Who kills Dexter in New Blood?
600.a: Harrison — his own son shoots him after Dexter kills Kurt Caldwell and confesses everything

600.q: What is the Bay Harbour Butcher case?
600.a: The FBI investigation into a series of dumped bodies in the bay — they are Dexter's victims

600.q: What is the deeper parallel between Dexter and Harry?
600.a: Harry trained Dexter to be a weapon against evil — but Harry killed himself when he saw what he had created

600.q: What is the significance of the plastic wrap and kill table?
600.a: It is a ritual — precise, controlled, and intimate. It allows Dexter to feel connected to his victims

600.q: Who is Hannah McKay?
600.a: A poisoner Dexter falls in love with in Season 7 — she flees with Harrison in the finale

600.q: What is the theme of the show's title sequence?
600.a: Dexter's morning routine — shaving, cooking eggs, tying shoelaces — all filmed to look like murder preparation

600.q: What is the Showtime revival's critical reception?
600.a: Dexter: New Blood was praised as a redemption after the original finale — Harrison's killing of Dexter was seen as fitting
